questions.question: 1 (a) Remove the lid from beaker A. Pour 150 cm 3 of hot water into beaker A and replace the lid. Place the thermometer in the water. parts.answer: sensible value for l and l S > l

5 × l values increasing all to the nearest mm. • axes labelled with quantity and unit • appropriate scales (occupying at least ½ grid) • plots all correct to ½ small square and precise plots • line well- j udged and thin and extended to axis L read correctly from graph W R in range 0.9 to 2.1 and with unit of N suspend load from loop of thread / any other suitable method to avoid standing load over marks on rule valid source of uncertainty e.g. test load not exactly 1.0 N / spring extension not linear / metre rule not uniform 0625/52 February/March 2022

parts.answer: θ for beaker A decreasing θ for beaker A in beaker B decreasing and recorded to at least 1 °C decreasing less quickly than for beaker A s, °C, °C all correct

statement matching readings in table comparison of temperature changes over 180s, matching st atement (need to see values used in justification) use conducting material / metal for beaker B / support beaker A in beaker B so that there is an air gap underneath too
2 suitable control variables e.g. initial temperature / volume of water / room temperature or other appropriate environmental condition
correct calculation of R unit °C / s 0625/52 February/March 2022 θ = 15 ± 1(°) separation sufficiently large

first line present, complete and straight second set of lines present, complete and straight in correct relation to first set β = 20° ± 2° statement matching results j ustification with correct values matching statement
one suitable precaution e.g. thin lines OR thin pencil / view bottom of pins OR keep pins upright / ensure pins far apart

difficult to align pins/place pins accurately, pins (too) thick mirror (too) thick 0625/52 February/March 2022 MP1 factor: valid factor which may affect rate of temperature rise MP2 apparatus: thermometer and additional apparatus necessary to measure independent variable MP3 method: • measure independent variable • measure temperature (change) and / or time appropriate to procedure • repeat for new value of independent variable MP4 control variable: any significant variable appropriate to independent variable (e.g. volume of water if current is the independent variable) MP5 table: columns, with units, for independent variable and dependent variable MP6 analysis: compare readings in the table to see if change in factor produces change in (rate of) temperature rise, plot (line) graph (with axes specified) MP7 additional point (one from) : 2nd valid control variable stated, at least 5 sets of data taken, repeat each measurement and take average,
